title: Moog Technology Successfully Steers Artemis II Launch
summary: Moog Inc. successfully provided critical technology for steering the Artemis II launch. The company's precision motion and control systems played a key role in the mission.

title: Carmel Ortiz on the evolving landscape of satellite communications
summary: Carmel Ortiz, SES's senior vice president of medium-Earth-orbit constellation programs, discusses new satellite communication technologies in a Space Minds interview with Mike Gruss. The conversation highlights advancements in the evolving satellite communications landscape.

title: Phantom Space buys thermal specialist to support orbital data center push
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies to support its Phantom Cloud orbital data center constellation. The acquisition aims to enhance thermal hardware capabilities for the planned constellation.

title: Relativity, Hermeus, Astrion and Divergent executives join Fortastra C-suite
summary: Fortastra, a Los Angeles-based startup, has appointed executives from Relativity Space, Hermeus, Astrion, and Divergent Technologies to its C-suite to develop maneuverable spacecraft for on-orbit security. The company aims to leverage their expertise in avionics, engineering, and product development.

title: Artemis 2 launches on first human mission to the moon in more than 50 years
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission launched four astronauts on the first human mission to the moon in over 50 years. This marks a significant milestone in space exploration beyond Earth's orbit.

title: SpaceX quietly files for big bang IPO
summary: SpaceX has confidentially filed for an IPO, potentially setting a record-breaking offering. This move is seen as a significant milestone for the space industry.

title: Teledyne forms dedicated space unit to capture rising demand
summary: Teledyne has established a dedicated space unit named Teledyne Space by consolidating its imaging, electronics, and component lines. This move aims to meet the increasing demand in the space industry.

title: Artemis 2’s (nearly) 10-day flight around the moon
summary: Artemis 2 will send humans around the moon for the first time in over 50 years, with a mission duration of nearly 10 days.

title: Saltzman: Space ‘baked into’ modern combat operations
summary: Gen. Saltzman emphasized the critical role of space in modern combat operations, with Space Force personnel deployed across the Middle East, Tampa, and South Carolina. He highlighted how space capabilities are now integral to military operations.

title: Aspect Aerospace secures early funding to advance swarm-deployable VLEO satellites
summary: Aspect Aerospace, a University of South Alabama spin-off, has secured $2.4 million in early funding to develop swarm-deployable very low Earth orbit (VLEO) satellites. These circuit-board-sized spacecraft aim to monitor the space environment.

title: Stunning new James Webb Space Telescope images reveal 'hidden' stars being born
summary: Astronomers used the James Webb Space Telescope to capture stunning new images of the star-forming region W51, revealing previously hidden details of stellar birth. The observations provide new insights into the processes of star formation.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon launch seen from space | Space photo of the day for April 2, 2026
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission launched on a historic journey around the moon and back, with the moment captured from space. This marks a significant milestone in lunar exploration.

title: How to track the Artemis 2 astronauts on their journey around the moon
summary: NASA has launched a website to allow real-time tracking of the Artemis 2 astronauts during their journey around the moon. This initiative provides public access to live updates on the mission.

title: Relive NASA's historic Artemis 2 launch to the moon in these stunning photos
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission, a historic lunar launch, is captured in stunning photos. The mission marks a significant step in space exploration.

title: April full moon 2026 dazzles as 'Pink Moon' lights up skies worldwide (photos)
summary: The April full moon in 2026 will be known as the 'Pink Moon,' illuminating skies worldwide with a stunning visual display. This event is purely astronomical and not tied to any space industry activity.

title: NASA just launched Artemis 2. What happens today could make or break the moon mission
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission is preparing for a crucial engine burn that will send four astronauts on a lunar flyby. The success of this maneuver is vital for the mission's future.

title: There was a bit of toilet trouble on NASA's Artemis 2 mission to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission to the moon encountered toilet troubles, though it was not the primary issue. The problem highlighted challenges for the mission's preparations.

title: Why is the Artemis 2 rocket launch different from all other rocket launches?
summary: The Artemis 2 rocket launch is notable for being the first crewed mission to the Moon since Apollo 17, marking a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program. It differs from other launches due to its planned lunar flyby and the involvement of international partners like ESA, CSA, and JAXA.

title: NASA launches 4 astronauts to the moon on historic Artemis 2 voyage, a lunar leap for the 21st century
summary: NASA launched the Artemis 2 mission, sending four astronauts toward the moon for the first time in over 50 years. This marks a significant milestone in lunar exploration for the 21st century.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' end credits showcase stunning nebula photos captured over 400 hours by a single astrophotographer — here's the inside story
summary: The end credits of 'Project Hail Mary' feature stunning nebula photos captured over 400 hours by a single astrophotographer. The project began with an Instagram message and culminated in a cinematic showcase.

title: Réunion Island Lava Reaches the Sea
summary: The February 2026 eruption at Réunion Island's Piton de la Fournaise volcano has been more prolonged and produced more lava than recent eruptions, with lava now reaching the sea.

title: Liftoff! NASA Launches Astronauts on Historic Artemis Moon Mission
summary: NASA successfully launched the Artemis II mission, marking the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years. The SLS rocket lifted off from Kennedy Space Center, carrying four astronauts on a historic journey around the Moon.

title: March of the Harmattan
summary: In March 2026, strong Harmattan winds carried Saharan dust across northwestern Africa and toward the Canary Islands, significantly reducing visibility and prompting public alerts.

title: Godspeed, Artemis II!
summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir shared a photo of the Artemis program patch from the International Space Station, highlighting preparations for the upcoming Artemis II mission to return humans to the Moon.

title: Landsat Reveals Reservoir Changes and Bathymetry
summary: Researchers used Landsat data to study reservoir changes and bathymetry in two recent studies. This highlights the utility of Landsat data in understanding water bodies.

title: Sendoff for Artemis II Crew
summary: NASA and CSA astronauts pose for a photo before the Artemis II crew proceed to a media event. The crew includes NASA astronauts Andre Douglas, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, Reid Wiseman, and CSA astronauts Jenni Gibbons and Jeremy Hansen.

title: Seeing Blue During Schirmacher’s Summer Melt Season
summary: A network of meltwater lakes and drainage channels has turned an Antarctic ice shelf, known for its blue ice areas, even bluer during the summer melt season.

title: I Am Artemis: Erik Richards
summary: Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager, discusses his role in supporting NASA's Artemis mission, highlighting his career in spacecraft communications. His journey reflects a lifelong passion for spaceflight, inspired by the Space Shuttle Program.

title: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
summary: NASA awarded Intuitive Machines a $180.4 million contract to deliver science and technology payloads to the Moon under the CLPS initiative and Artemis program. The mission will carry seven payloads, including five from NASA, to enhance lunar surface understanding.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: Artemis II is set to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration following the Apollo 17 mission in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz lifting off from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is involved in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights the company's role in advancing space exploration.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit from California aboard the Transporter 16 mission. This marks another successful rideshare mission for the company.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company is yet to announce a new launch date.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. The mission was revealed by Administrator Jared Isaacman and other NASA leaders.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on upgrades to its Starbase launch pads in preparation for the upcoming Block 3 Starship launches. This effort highlights the company's focus on increasing launch frequency.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets carrying internet satellites. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the ISS from a repaired launch pad at Baikonur Cosmodrome, following a previous Soyuz mission. The launch occurred nearly four months after Soyuz MS-28's liftoff from the same site.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed with versatile capabilities for space applications. The innovation could significantly enhance satellite and spacecraft performance.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team celebrates the successful recovery of the spacecraft.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 ministerial meeting in November 2025, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference while being cost-effective and lightweight. This innovation could replace bulky and expensive antennas currently used.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies, a company specializing in advanced satellite thermal components for in-space applications.

title: Artemis II Is On The Way To The Moon
summary: Artemis II is en route to the Moon with NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Christina Koch, Victor Glover, and Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen. Koch and Glover will be the first woman and person of color to reach the Moon, while Hansen becomes the first non-American on the mission.

title: Exclusive: In-Orbit Manufacturing Startup Dispatch Emerges from Stealth
summary: Dispatch, an in-orbit manufacturing startup, has emerged from stealth with $500,000 in funding. The company plans to build an uncrewed space station to host manufacturing infrastructure in orbit.

title: Antaris Raises $28M Series A
summary: Antaris has secured $28 million in a Series A funding round led by WestWave Capital, with additional investment from Lockheed Martin Ventures and other new investors.

title: Top Space Lawmaker on Moon Base, Artemis Plans
summary: A top space lawmaker expressed strong support for establishing a Moon base as part of Artemis plans, suggesting it could serve as a strategic launch point for future Mars missions.

title: Voyager and Icarus Team Up on ISS Robotics Demo
summary: Voyager and Icarus are collaborating on a robotics demonstration aboard the International Space Station (ISS), marking a significant step in space-based automation and technology testing. This partnership highlights the growing role of private companies in advancing ISS capabilities.

title: Q&A With Lunar Base Manager Carlos Garcia-Galan
summary: Carlos Garcia-Galan transitioned from managing NASA's lunar-orbiting habitat program to overseeing the agency's rapid lunar surface base initiative. He was interviewed during NASA's Ignition event about his new role.

title: Transporter-16 Sends 119 Payloads to Orbit
summary: The Transporter-16 mission successfully launched 119 payloads to orbit. This marks the latest in a series of rideshare missions to space.

title: Starcloud Raises $170M Series A at $1.1B Valuation
summary: Starcloud secured $170 million in a Series A funding round, valuing the company at $1.1 billion. The investment underscores investor interest in orbital data-center ventures and Starcloud's proven flight heritage.

title: Bellatrix Raises $20M For In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace raised $20 million for its in-space propulsion technology. The company aims to scale production after flight-qualifying its core technologies.

title: NASA Launches Artemis II in Further Step Toward a Moon Return
summary: NASA successfully launched Artemis II, the first crewed mission of the Artemis program, to test the Orion spacecraft's life support systems. This mission marks a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon.

title: CanarySat CEO Antonio Abad Outlines the Sovereign, Secure Approach Behind the Magec Constellation
summary: CanarySat, a new satellite operator backed by Arquimea and based in Spain's Canary Islands, unveiled its Magec constellation, a Ka-band Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) network focused on sovereign and secure satellite services. The announcement was made during SATShow Week.

title: Bloomberg, CNBC Report SpaceX Submitted Confidential Filing for IPO
summary: SpaceX has submitted a confidential filing for its initial public offering (IPO) with the SEC, as reported by Bloomberg and CNBC. This allows the company to prepare its IPO registration privately before going public.

title: Measat Appoints New CTO
summary: Measat Global appointed John Loke as its new Chief Technology Officer to lead the company's digital evolution and regional strategy.

title: Live in the Booth: Arianespace CEO David Cavaillolès Previews Ariane 6 Ramp-Up
summary: Arianespace is progressing with its launch campaign for Amazon's Leo project after its first mission in February, with the next launch scheduled for April. CEO David Cavaillolès discussed the ramp-up of the Ariane 6 rocket during a live event.

title: Faced With Starlink Competition, Ground Segment Leaders Turn to Orchestration, Specialization
summary: Ground segment leaders in the space industry are adapting to competition from LEO megaconstellations like Starlink by focusing on orchestration and specialization. This shift is driven by the need to innovate in technology and services to remain competitive.

title: Delta Taps Amazon Leo for IFC Rollout on 500 Aircraft
summary: Delta Air Lines has partnered with Amazon to deploy Amazon Leo in-flight connectivity (IFC) service on 500 aircraft starting in 2028. This collaboration marks a significant expansion of Delta's IFC offerings.

title: Air Force Awards BlackSky $99M to Large Aperture Optical Payload for Space-Based Imaging
summary: The U.S. Air Force awarded BlackSky a $99 million contract to accelerate the design and development of a large aperture optical payload for space-based imaging. This contract was received in early March and aims to enhance imaging capabilities.

title: Claussen Departs, ST Engineering iDirect Names Sridhar Kuppanna as CEO
summary: ST Engineering iDirect has appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as CEO, succeeding Don Claussen who led the company for three years. The move highlights a leadership transition within the satellite communications firm.

title: Es’hailSat and EgyptSat Sign VSAT Partnership Deal
summary: Es’hailSat and EgyptSat have signed a multi-year VSAT partnership deal to enhance connectivity services in the Middle East. The collaboration aims to improve regional satellite communication infrastructure.
